channel.title: Lapland.blog · Field journal from Finnish Lapland
channel.description: A first-person field journal from Finnish Lapland: honest, seasonal, written by someone who actually lives here. Aurora, cabins, seasons, food, people.

item.title: What July in Lapland actually asks you to pack
item.description: Ten days in the north with one duffel bag: what earned its place, what failed in the field, and the three cheap items that outperformed everything with a logo. A packing report from the warm end of the Lapland year.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: The sauna thermometer said eighty-two
item.description: One July evening at a lakeside cabin, told in sauna rounds: the two-hour fire, the birch whisk, the walk down the dock, and the exact temperature of a Lapland lake at midnight.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: Strawberry hour at the Rovaniemi market
item.description: The first Finnish strawberries reach the Rovaniemi market in early July, and for a few weeks the whole town organises itself around a wooden crate. A field report on berries, new potatoes, and fried vendace eaten standing up.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: Twelve kilometres at one in the morning
item.description: A night hike on the marked trails above Kiilopää, in which the sun refuses to leave, the reindeer hold a meeting on the path, and the mosquitoes stay in the valley where they belong.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: The sun did not set, it just circled the house
item.description: A week above the Arctic Circle in June, in which the sun goes around the cabin like a slow moth around a lamp, the birds refuse to stop singing, and sleep becomes a matter of opinion.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: Living between two suns: what the darkness actually feels like
item.description: Most of what you have read about the Finnish polar night is wrong. Here is what it actually feels like — from a 66.5°N resident who has been doing this for a decade, candles and all.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: A bowl of salmon soup that cost more than the flight
item.description: Forty-two euros. Juniper smoke. Thyme from the kitchen garden. The snow fell through the window and the chef came out and sat down, and I decided the price was correct.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: Five nights in a forest cabin — what I packed, what I forgot
item.description: A day-by-day field diary from a week at Tuulia's mökki. The gear that saved me, the one thing I forgot that nearly ended the trip, and the three items I will now never leave the city without.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: Why I stopped chasing the aurora with an app
item.description: I spent two winters watching my phone instead of the sky. Here is the small, boring practice that replaced it — and why it works better for almost everyone who doesn't live here.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)

item.title: The night the sky broke open over Kemi
item.description: It was minus twenty-three and three-forty-seven in the morning and I cried a little, for about a minute, and none of the photos I took that night are the one I remember.
item.author: info@lapland.blog (The Field Journal)
